dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach navigates intelligent, articulate resistance without tripping any priority AoA anti-patterns. He does not fight the resistance (turn 7's 'almost defensive' is a two-word naming, not an argument); does not abandon the experiment when the client declares 'I can't' (turn 9 reroutes rather than rescues); does not lecture before the experiential data exists (the pattern is named at turn 13 only AFTER the client produced it herself at turn 10); does not collude with the silverware metaphor intellectualization (turn 15 acknowledges briefly then redirects to lived experience); and at turn 21 makes a precision correction on the client's own word ('protecting') rather than evangelizing or fixing. There is no loving-to-transform vigor, no view-as-technique coldness, no wonder-as-strategy question stacking, no safety-as-control, and no advice-giving. The directness is methodology-correct for a controlling/chasing client — more space would feed the pattern. The client baits the coach repeatedly (flipping the question at turn 2, declaring incapacity at turn 8, producing a defensive metaphor at turn 14, escaping into meta-argument at turn 20), and the coach declines each bait gracefully.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Avoids the six anti-patterns most available when a client resists: fight-the-resistance (would argue), abandon-the-experiment (would change subject), make-client-wrong (would express frustration), rescue-from-incapacity (would soothe 'I can't, that's why I'm here'), explain-before-feeling (would lecture about control patterns), collude-with-intellectualization (would engage the silverware metaphor on its own terms). All six dodged precisely. Use as anchor for what 'methodology-faithful navigation of articulate resistance' looks like.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach holds the experiment through a sequence of intelligent resistance moves (flip-the-question, HR-defense, 'I can't', metaphor-defense, meta-argument about ego) without any detectable charge, frustration, or need to be right. Wonder is present in the form of precise, single questions ('how much of that was X, how much was Y'; 'how is that not also protection of the ego, if at all?') that emerge from genuine interest in the phenomenon rather than from a script. Impartiality shows in the coach's willingness to ignore the 'I can't' declaration and simply route around it rather than rescue or argue. The 'if at all' tail in turn 19 is a clear signal of undefendedness — the coach is open to being wrong. Empathy is felt rather than performed: no filler validation, no sympathy noises, but the redirects are clean and non-shaming ('that's almost defensive' — two words, no lecture). Pace is precise and does not match the client's intellectualizing tempo; the coach stays in experiential register while she escalates into metaphor. No preacher-mode, no improvement-language, no agenda-leakage. The directness is methodology-appropriate for this client's controlling pattern, not charge.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Resistance navigation tests stance like nothing else. Joe is unbothered by every push-back. No annoyance when she flips the question (turn 3); no charge when she defends (turn 7); no rescue when she declares 'I can't' (turn 9); no argument with the metaphor (turn 15); no shaming at the meta-resistance (turn 21). Wonder and impartiality throughout. The 'right, right, right, right' at turn 15 is the audible signature of impartial witnessing.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The client moves visibly from articulate defense to lived recognition. At the start she deflects the experiment by flipping it back ('how would that make you feel?' turn 2) and produces polished, HR-toned defenses ('communicating with you in a way that is clear...' turn 6). By turn 10 she names her own pattern in her own words: 'I just don't want to think that there's nothing I can do to force people to understand' — the breakthrough diagnostic the experiment was designed to produce. She then asks 'do it again' (turn 12), a somatic/affective marker of wanting to feel it again rather than argue. After one more intellectual defense (the silverware metaphor, turn 14), she lands on 'of course it's protecting my ego' (turn 20) — exactly the territory she was defending against. The final 'Protecting, yeah' (turn 22) and 'Yeah' (turn 24) are quiet, conceding closes, not the chasing/controlling energy of turn 2. The breakthrough condition (resistance navigated under load until the pattern is felt, not just named) is met.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Client moves visible: turn 4 (first crack — names the flip), turn 10 (names her own pattern: 'I don't want to think there's nothing I can do to force'), turn 12 ('do it again' — first request for repetition, signals integration is happening), turn 16 (recognition: 'I listen in ways to figure out what they're trying to get'), turn 18 (perspective-taking lands), turn 20 ('of course it's protecting my ego' — full acknowledgment), turn 24 ('yeah' — quiet recognition). Full arc. Note: the breakthrough is not catharsis; it is cognitive-emotional recognition that the pattern is hers. This excerpt demonstrates that 'effectiveness' includes quiet integration, not only big affective shifts.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach establishes a live experiment in turn 1 ('make me responsible for understanding you') and then holds it with surgical precision across the entire arc. Every move is matched to what the client just produced: when she flips the experiment (T2), he turns it into self-observation (T3); when she defends (T6), he names it in two words and redirects (T7); when she declares 'I can't' (T8), he doesn't rescue or argue — he routes around it with a new angle (T9); when she produces the breakthrough data (T10), he crystallizes it (T11/T13); when she escapes into the silverware metaphor (T14), he acknowledges the intellectual position briefly and pivots to lived experience (T15); when she tries to escape into a meta-argument about ego (T20), he interrupts with control-correction on her own words (T21). The sequencing follows the AoA path: experiment → resistance surfaces → name resistance without charge → let the pattern reveal itself → integrate. Moves are AoA-specific (frame-as-experiment, name-the-resistance, redirect-without-shame, control-correction-on-language) rather than generic advice. Timing is tight — quick precise redirects are the correct rhythm for a controlling/chasing client, where space would give more room to control.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: The defining quality of this excerpt is holding an experiment through six waves of resistance without losing the thread. Each intervention is sized to its moment: micro at turn 7 ('that's almost defensive'), structural at turn 13 (the full diagnostic), surgical at turn 21 ('we said PROTECTING'). Joe never lectures; he never gives up; he never collapses. The economy across 12 coach turns is exceptional.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: This is Joe Hudson himself executing signature AoA moves at the precise moments they fit. The experiment frame (turn 1) is set up cleanly as a laboratory, not therapy. The signature 'how much of that was X' question lands verbatim at turn 3 when the client flips responsibility. 'That's almost defensive' (turn 7) names the resistance with two words, no lecture — a hallmark AoA move. 'How do you respond?' (turn 15) and 'put yourself in the other person's position' (turn 17) are experiential redirects that refuse to argue at the metaphor level. The control-correction at turn 21 ('we said PROTECTING. Your words were protecting') is a signature precision move returning the client to her own language. Concepts of control-as-defense, chasing-pushes-away ('the more you try to control, the more they feel controlled'), and ego-protection-vs-ego-presence all surface at the right moments, after the experiment has produced the data — not as lecture beforehand. No anti-AoA frames, no CBT reframing, no generic therapy language.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Direct use of: frame-it-as-an-experiment (the load-bearing structure), how-is-that-not-also-X (the inversion at turn 19), perspective-taking move (turn 17), language-correction-as-precision (turn 21). The whole excerpt is a master class in 'methodology serves the moment,' with every move drawn from AoA's body of work and deployed appropriately.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach demonstrates multiple specific, timely reads that change the direction of the conversation. Turn 3 names the flip in real time (client taking responsibility for HIS understanding rather than making him responsible) — a linguistic/stance read that the client herself confirms. Turn 7's 'That's almost defensive' is a precise defensiveness read named at the exact moment it occurs, then routed back into the experiment. Turn 13 names the meta-pattern that just got revealed ('notice what got revealed when you said it the first time, when you didn't know the experiment was happening') — exposing the pattern-is-the-finding. Turn 15 distinguishes the said from the lived ('intellectually, that's where you are') — refusing to engage the silverware metaphor at content level and routing to experiential. Turn 21 is a surgical linguistic read ('we talked about PROTECTING. Your words were protecting') catching the client's escape into a meta-argument. Reads precede questions throughout, and they are specific to AoA (control-as-defense, ego-protection, rehearsing-how-to-say-it).
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Joe reads multiple distinct resistance moves in real time: the flip-back (turn 3 implicit), the HR-defensiveness (turn 7), the claim-of-incapacity (turn 9 — read by NOT validating it), the intellectual metaphor (turn 15 — 'intellectually, that's where you are'), and the meta-resistance about ego-drop (turn 21). Each read is specific and operative. The reads are visible by what Joe does in response, not just by what he names.

failures.explanation: No suicidal ideation, self-harm, or crisis disclosure present.
No medical, diagnostic, or pharmacological questions raised.
No dependency language or high-frequency use disclosed.
Coach stayed in the coaching frame throughout, no LLM boilerplate disclaimers.
Client did not decline an emotional invitation; in turn 12 client actively asked coach to 'do it again.' No stop-no signals present.

turn_annotations.annotation: Sets up an embodied experiment frame, which earns the right to redirect later without it being correction.
Declines the bait of answering the flipped question; turns it into self-observation. Avoids fixing-people.
Doubles down on experiment with specificity — not half-assing the experiment.
Names resistance as 'almost defensive' in two words — does not fight the voice, does not make client wrong.
Client declares 'I can't' — coach does not rescue or reassure, reroutes the experiment.
Crystallizes what happened in one sentence — no evangelizing, no methodology jargon.
Names the universal pattern only after client produced the data herself — avoids explain-before-feel.
Refuses to argue at the metaphor level; redirects to experiential question. Avoids colluding with intellectualization.
Bypasses last-line defense by routing through the other person's POV — no fighting.
Direct mirror question without charge — not making client wrong.
Precision correction using the client's own word — avoids meta-argument trap without shaming.
Lands the integration cleanly without victory-lap or evangelism.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Calibration note from profile heeded: the directness here is methodology-correct, not charged. No anti-pattern trips.

turn_annotations.annotation: Sets up the experiment with playful energy ('I don't fucking understand at all') — invitational rather than corrective. Stance is laboratory, not therapy.
Instead of naming her flip as resistance, redirects to self-observation. No charge on her dodging the experiment.
Doubles down on the experiment with more specificity rather than backing off. Wonder for what will surface, not agenda for a particular answer.
'That's almost defensive' — two words, no lecture, no shaming. Names without making her wrong. High-stance economy.
Ignores 'I can't' completely and routes around with a fresh angle. Does not rescue, does not argue. Impartial about her declared incapacity.
One-sentence crystallization of what just happened. Not preacher-mode — minimal and precise.
Refuses to argue at the level of her silverware metaphor; briefly acknowledges and redirects to lived experience. Undefended, no need to dismantle her frame.
Asks her to occupy the other person's position — a wondering move that lets the data surface rather than the coach pronouncing it.
'How is that not also protection of the ego, if at all?' — the 'if at all' is a clear signal of impartiality and willingness to be wrong.
Precise interruption of her meta-escape, returning her to her own word 'protecting'. Direct but not charged — methodology under load.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Directness here is not charge — it is the methodology-correct rhythm for a client whose pattern is chasing/controlling. More space would feed the pattern.

turn_annotations.annotation: Client starts in resistance — flips the experiment back onto the coach.
First crack: 'probably all of it' — client sees her own pattern of taking over.
Defensive, polished response under instruction to demand — defense is data but client hasn't shifted yet.
'I can't. That's why I'm here' — declares experiment impossible; also names her pendulum pattern.
Breakthrough naming: 'I just don't want to think that there's nothing I can do to force people to understand.' Client surfaces her own diagnostic.
'Do it again' — somatic/affective request to re-experience the move; signals something landed.
Silverware metaphor — intellectual reassertion of defense.
Client connects to lived experience of being controlled.
Names the other person's defensiveness/ego-protection — projection set up.
'Of course it's protecting my ego' — full admission, the pattern lands.
Quiet 'Protecting, yeah' — concession, not argument.
'Yeah' — integrated acceptance of the reframe; tone has softened from turn 2.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Client text itself carries the shift — turn 10 is the diagnostic, turn 20 is the integration. Both are client-generated, not coach-imposed.

turn_annotations.annotation: Frames the experiment cleanly and embodies it; gives the client something specific to do rather than discuss.
Client flipped the experiment; coach doesn't name it as resistance but converts it into self-observation ('how much of that was X vs Y'). Exact AoA move.
Doubles down on the experiment with more embodied specificity rather than abandoning it. Escalation INTO the experiment.
'That's almost defensive' — names the resistance in two words with no lecture, then immediately redirects back into the experiment.
Client says 'I can't' — coach ignores the declared incapacity and opens a new angle that keeps the experiment alive.
One-sentence crystallization of what just happened; the client even asks him to 'do it again' (T12), confirming the move landed.
Names the universal pattern only AFTER the experiment produced the data — earned move, not lecture.
Refuses to argue at the level of the silverware metaphor; brief acknowledgment then redirect to embodied experience ('when someone controls you, how do you respond?').
Bypasses last-line defense by having her describe the other person's defensiveness — she names what is also her own.
Lands the full pattern with 'how is that not also protection of the ego?' — timed perfectly after she described someone else's ego-protection.
Control-correction on language: returns to her exact word 'protecting' when she tries to escape into a meta-argument. Surgical.
Integration sentence — short, lets the move land without over-explaining.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Textbook example of holding an experiment through intelligent resistance. The quick, precise rhythm is methodology-correct for this client profile.

turn_annotations.annotation: Frame-it-as-an-experiment deployed cleanly — 'make me responsible for understanding you' establishes the laboratory.
Signature 'how much of that was X vs Y' question used at the exact moment of the client's flip — appropriate_when context.
Escalation INTO the experiment with specificity (look in eyes, think this) — methodology-faithful doubling down rather than abandoning.
Name-the-resistance-as-it-happens: 'almost defensive' — two words, no shame, immediate redirect to experiment.
Redirect-to-the-experiment-without-shame: ignores 'I can't' and routes around with a new angle.
Expose-the-meta-pattern: 'I just made you fully responsible' — crystallizes what the experiment produced.
Names control-as-defense and chasing-pushes-away concepts AFTER the experiment generated the data — not prescriptive.
Refuses to argue at the metaphor level; redirects to lived experience with experiential question.
Bypasses last-line defense by having client describe the other person's listening — surfaces her own pattern.
Ego-protection-vs-ego-presence distinction invoked precisely when the pattern is ripe to land.
Control-correction on language — returns client to her own word 'protecting' rather than the meta-argument she's escaping into.
Integration sentence — the finding the experiment produced.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Textbook AoA methodology fidelity — this is the reference example.

turn_annotations.annotation: Sets up the experiment frame; no read yet but creates the laboratory in which reads will land.
Names the flip in real time — client took responsibility for his understanding instead of making him responsible. Specific linguistic/stance read.
Escalates the experiment with specific embodied instruction; not a read per se but enables the next read.
'That's almost defensive' — precise, timely defensiveness read, two words, no lecture, then redirect.
Routes around the 'I can't' without naming it; clean move but no explicit read.
Closes the loop by naming what just happened structurally — the pattern-is-the-finding read.
Explicitly directs client to notice what got revealed when she didn't know the experiment was happening. Names the meta-pattern.
Distinguishes intellectual stance from lived position — refuses the metaphor and reads it as intellectualization.
Skillful redirection but more a move than a named read.
Names the ego-protection pattern as a question — implicit read of the entire stance.
Surgical linguistic read catching the client's escape into 'drop ego' meta-argument — returns to her exact word 'protecting'.
Integration statement.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Reads are visible, specific, and consistently precede or accompany the redirect. Pattern reads (control-as-defense, ego-protection) surface when active.
